title: HTML to Text
shortDescription: Extract readable plain text from HTML snippets and full documents.
metaDescription: Turn HTML into readable plain text in your browser. Paste snippets or full documents and copy the extracted text in seconds.
intro: Paste HTML and get clean plain text back instantly in the browser.
overview:
Use this tool when you need the words from HTML without the tags. It strips markup and leaves behind readable text you can copy into notes, tickets, editors, or internal tools.

It handles short snippets and larger documents, so you can check how content will read once formatting is gone. The result is plain text only, which makes it easier to reuse in downstream workflows without carrying over HTML structure.

Because the conversion runs locally, your input stays in the browser while the text is processed.
howItWorks: Paste HTML into the input field.
Tags are removed and text content is preserved.
Copy the plain-text result or continue working with it.

examples.description: Line breaks and list items become separate text lines.

faq.question: Does this tool keep links or image alt text?
faq.answer: It keeps the visible text inside elements, but it does not preserve HTML attributes as structured output.

faq.question: What happens to scripts, styles, and comments?
faq.answer: They are not included in the plain-text result, so the output focuses on visible content.

faq.question: Why does spacing sometimes look different from the source?
faq.answer: HTML layout and text layout are not the same. The tool converts structure into readable lines, but exact visual spacing is not preserved.

faq.question: Can I rely on the output for legal or technical formatting?
faq.answer: Use it for readable extraction, not for exact rendering. If spacing, tables, or indentation matter, review the result before reuse.
